name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Java I/O
description: An Enterprise JavaBean uses the java.io package to access the file system, violating the EJB specification. File system APIs are not well-suited for business components; resource manager APIs such as JDBC should be used instead for data access.
detection_pattern: STRUCTURAL node within an EJB context that performs file I/O operations via EXTERNAL calls to java.io classes. Look for STORAGE or EXTERNAL nodes accessing the file system directly from within bean code instead of using container-managed resource APIs.
